commit.message:
[Filebeat] Fix flaky filestream delete retry integration subtest (#50037)

* filebeat: stabilize filestream delete retry test

Use require.Eventually to wait for the async remove retry counter to reach the expected value before asserting. This removes a race where the assertion can run before the second retry increments the counter.

commit.message:
feat(starrocks): manage catalog, roles, and OIDC chain in substructure (#4460)

* feat(starrocks): manage catalog, roles, and OIDC chain in substructure

Move StarRocks post-deploy SQL setup from a manual export to
programmatic management in the substructure stack using pulumi-command
local.Command resources executed against the FE MySQL port (9030) via
the internal NLB.

Changes:
- Add pulumi-command>=1.0,<2 dependency
- substructure/starrocks/__main__.py:
  - Simplify Vault role creation statements to CREATE USER +
    GRANT <role> TO USER, delegating privilege definitions to native
    StarRocks roles
  - Add catalog-setup Command: idempotent CREATE EXTERNAL CATALOG IF
    NOT EXISTS + ALTER CATALOG SET for the ol_data_lake_iceberg Glue
    catalog (conditional on enable_data_lake_integration)
  - Add roles-setup Command: CREATE ROLE IF NOT EXISTS + full GRANT
    definitions for readonly/app/admin; Iceberg catalog grants appended
    when data lake is enabled; depends_on catalog-setup when applicable
  - Add oidc-setup Command: ADMIN SET FRONTEND CONFIG to toggle the
    authentication_chain (conditional on oidc_enabled, currently false)
  - All commands use MYSQL_PWD/STARROCKS_HOST env vars to keep
    credentials out of the command string and Pulumi state; multi-
    statement SQL piped via stdin using printf '%s' "$STARROCKS_SQL"
  - SHA-256 hash triggers ensure re-execution on any SQL change
- applications/starrocks/__main__.py:
  - Remove iceberg_catalog_sql stack export (now managed by
    substructure); update comment to reflect new ownership
- Stack YAML files (QA + Production): add enable_data_lake_integration,
  aws_region, oidc_enabled config keys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(starrocks): remove ALTER CATALOG — statement does not exist in StarRocks

StarRocks has no ALTER CATALOG statement for updating catalog properties.
The CREATE EXTERNAL CATALOG IF NOT EXISTS is already idempotent for
first-run; property changes require a manual DROP CATALOG followed by
pulumi up to recreate. Update comments to document this constraint.

The AI agent that flagged this suggested ALTER CATALOG SET PROPERTIES
as the fix, but that syntax also does not exist in StarRocks — the
Catalog SQL reference only includes CREATE/DROP/SET/SHOW commands with
no ALTER variant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(sdk): namespace improvements for deepagents (#2386)

# Namespace factory refactor

This PR changes `StoreBackend`'s `namespace` factory to receive a
`Runtime` instance directly instead of a `BackendContext` wrapper.
Factories now look like `lambda rt: (rt.server_info.user.identity,
"fs")` rather than `lambda ctx: (ctx.runtime.context.user_id, "fs")`.

Backwards compatibility is preserved via `_NamespaceRuntimeCompat`,
which duck-types as both `Runtime` and the legacy `BackendContext` and
emits `DeprecationWarning`s when `.runtime` or `.state` is accessed.
`BackendContext` and the old-style callable signature are deprecated and
will be removed in v0.7. Tests across the backends and middleware suites
were updated to the new signature.

docs update: https://github.com/langchain-ai/docs/pull/3496/changes

---------

Co-authored-by: Mason Daugherty <mason@langchain.dev>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: eyurtsev <3205522+eyurtsev@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Deduplicate peer addresses within PEERS batch before dialing

A PEERS message containing the same address repeated N times would
temporarily reserve N outbound pool slots before the spawned tasks
discovered the duplicates and released them. This allowed a malicious
peer to transiently exhaust outbound slots.

Fix: deduplicate addresses in add_peers() before processing.

Closes #1190

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(SM-18): Delete lookupFuzzy, lookupFuzzyCallable, globalIndex, callableIndex (#769)

* Initial plan

* Update test files for SymbolTable interface changes

Remove lookupFuzzy, lookupFuzzyCallable, globalIndex, and callableIndex
references from all test files. Replace lookupFuzzyCallable with
lookupCallableByName. Update getStats assertions to only expect
{ fileCount }. Remove tests that exclusively tested removed methods.

Files updated:
- symbol-table.test.ts: Remove lookupFuzzy describe block and all
  globalIndex/callableIndex tests, update callable method references
- symbol-resolver.test.ts: Remove SM-16 lookupFuzzy test block,
  update Tier 3 describe title
- type-env.test.ts: Update all mock SymbolTable objects and spy
  variable names
- call-form.test.ts: Update ownerId propagation test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(SM-18): Remove lookupFuzzy, lookupFuzzyCallable, globalIndex, callableIndex

Remove from SymbolTable interface and implementation:
- lookupFuzzy method
- lookupFuzzyCallable method
- globalIndex Map
- callableIndex Map (renamed to callableByName, backing lookupCallableByName)

Add lookupCallableByName as the targeted replacement for fuzzy callable
lookups. Migrate all production callers:
- resolution-context.ts: lookupFuzzyCallable → lookupCallableByName
- type-env.ts: lookupFuzzyCallable → lookupCallableByName
- call-processor.ts: lookupFuzzy → lookupCallableByName (D2 widen paths)

Remove fuzzyCallCount/fuzzyCallableCallCount stats and globalSymbolCount
from getStats(). Update pipeline.ts logging accordingly.

Memory savings: globalIndex stored every non-Property symbol (typically
the largest index by entry count). Removing it eliminates one Map plus
all its per-name arrays — net savings proportional to unique symbol
count in the project.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/4a658c69-41a9-4d57-8527-50ca544ca967

* fix(SM-18): address all PR #769 review findings

1. type-env.test.ts mock: add missing lookupImplByName + getFiles methods.

2. Macro/Delegate tests: 2 new tests confirm C/C++ Macro and C# Delegate
   are indexed in callableByName.

3. D2 widen path test: module-alias scenario verifying lookupCallableByName
   resolves methods in aliased files that shadow same-file definitions.

4. CALLABLE_TYPES unified: exported from symbol-table.ts (single source of
   truth), imported in call-processor.ts. Removed duplicate
   CALLABLE_SYMBOL_TYPES constant.

5. getStats() observability restored: tier hit counters (tierSameFile,
   tierImportScoped, tierGlobal, tierMiss) replace the removed
   fuzzyCallCount diagnostic.

* chore(SM-18): remove unnecessary `as any` casts on valid NodeLabel types

Macro, Delegate, TypeAlias, Const, and Variable are all valid NodeLabel
values in gitnexus-shared. The casts suppressed type checking without
purpose and signaled false uncertainty.

---------

commit.message:
Reserve pending peer_id during HELLO to prevent duplicate handshake DoS

Previously, peer_id dedup was checked only after the full handshake
completed (in handle_accepted_inbound_peer). This allowed N concurrent
connections from the same peer_id to all occupy pending slots during
the AUTH phase.

Fix: insert peer_id into PendingConnections.by_peer_id during
handshake() immediately after recv_hello() reveals the remote identity.
Duplicate peer_ids are rejected with PeerDuplicate error before AUTH.
Cleanup on handshake failure removes the reservation. Matches
stellar-core Peer::recvHello() which checks getPendingPeers().

Closes #1189

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update SDK to 0.2.1, increase timeout to 240s, update story files and models

- Migrate to github-copilot-sdk 0.2.1: update imports and API calls
  (PermissionHandler moved to copilot.session, create/resume_session
  now use keyword args, session.send() takes plain string)
- Increase _send_and_wait timeout from 120s to 240s
- Update hosts/dnd/stories campaign files
- Remove old models, add bgdia.txt, cm.txt, gos.txt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set API ingress to INGRESS_TRAFFIC_ALL

Cloud Run-to-Cloud Run calls over the public internet URL are treated as
external traffic. INGRESS_TRAFFIC_INTERNAL_ONLY was blocking all requests
from the UI BFF and returning 404.

Access control is enforced via IAM: osrs-ui-sa holds roles/run.invoker
on the API service. Cloud Run IAM auth rejects unauthorized callers at
the load balancer before the container is invoked.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: promote workflow - dynamic lakehouse discovery + update stale notebooks

Three fixes for the 'Enforce Schemas in Target Workspace' job failure:

1. discover_fabric_schema.py: When no LAKEHOUSE_NAME is set, try all
   lakehouses in the workspace instead of just the first one. Picks the
   first lakehouse that has tables (fixes 0-table discovery when
   DataCenterLakehouse is empty but DigitalRealty_Capacity has data).

2. promote workflow enforce-schemas: Replace hardcoded 'DataCenterLakehouse'
   lookup with dynamic resolution - first tries matching the lakehouse
   name from schema discovery, then falls back to first available.

3. promote workflow HTTP 400 fallback: When notebook upload returns 400,
   now updates the existing notebook's definition before triggering it,
   so it runs fresh code instead of stale cached code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Flask web app with Plotly heatmap showing estimated 1RM

Uses modified Epley formula from workout-temple.com.

commit.message:
Deploy: 更新博客内容 2026-04-10 21:53:07

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(auth): add Express middleware for JWT auth, disable Wonderwall autoLogin

Replace Wonderwall autoLogin with Express-level JWT middleware to allow
M2M traffic from KISS while enforcing auth on all other routes.

Middleware behavior:
- Health checks (/api/isalive, /api/isready): pass through
- M2M routes (/api/v1/*): pass through (own introspection-based auth)
- Valid user JWT: pass through (validates signature, issuer, audience, expiry)
- App tokens (idtyp=app): rejected on non-M2M routes (defense-in-depth)
- Browser requests without token: redirect to /oauth2/login
- API requests without token: 401 Unauthorized
- Development without NAIS cluster: pass through

NAIS config: autoLogin: true → false

121 new tests: 23 middleware unit tests + 98 per-endpoint enforcement tests.
Reviewed by GPT 5.4, Codex, Sonnet, and Opus (2 rounds).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve 13 security vulnerabilities across all tools

Critical:
- Replace pickle.loads() with JSON serialization in embed.py (arbitrary code exec)
- Set config file permissions to 0o600 and prefer env vars for API keys

High:
- Fix SQL injection: parameterized queries in query-session.py
- Fix TOCTOU race condition: atomic lock creation in watch-sessions.py
- Fix path traversal: validate WSL paths in sync-knowledge.py

Medium:
- FTS5 query injection: sanitize special chars/operators (query-session.py, briefing.py)
- Silent error handling: add stderr logging to ~23 bare except blocks
- Input validation: length limits for titles, content, and filters
- Whitelist validation for f-string DDL in migrations
- Database integrity check (PRAGMA quick_check) on open
- Backup verification with SHA-256 checksums

Added:
- test_security.py: 9 security verification tests
- SECURITY.md: security policy and best practices

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix workflow summary: accurate Duration display and Node.js 20 deprecation warning (#2394)

* fix: accurate workflow summary duration and update codecov-action to v6

- Fix Duration field showing zeros in workflow summary by computing
  wallclock time from allure-results/*.json timestamps (max(stop) -
  min(start)) instead of relying on Allure 3 summary.json which
  reports near-zero durations for parallel test runs
- Update codecov/codecov-action from v5 to v6 to eliminate Node.js 20
  deprecation warnings (v6 targets Node.js 24 internally)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ShaftHQ/SHAFT_ENGINE/sessions/a4228e17-6045-4bbd-9b84-565c7b622ee2

Co-authored-by: MohabMohie <19201898+MohabMohie@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use GitHub Actions job wallclock time for summary duration

Replace jq-based allure-results scanning with a consistent, cross-platform
approach tied to the actual GitHub Actions job timeline:

- setup-test-env: record job start time (date +%s) to GITHUB_ENV as
  SHAFT_JOB_STARTED_S before any SDK setup
- post-test-report: compute DURATION = (now - SHAFT_JOB_STARTED_S) * 1000ms,
  which matches the GitHub-tracked job timing; guard against negative values;
  warn when env var is absent (e.g. if setup-test-env was not called)
- Eliminates the jq dependency and fixes near-zero durations on parallel runs
- Also updates codecov/codecov-action v5 → v6 (Node.js 24)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ShaftHQ/SHAFT_ENGINE/sessions/d709535b-5926-4695-96f1-3b733345bf31

Co-authored-by: MohabMohie <19201898+MohabMohie@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add NODE_AUTH_TOKEN to npm publish step

The publish step was missing NODE_AUTH_TOKEN, causing authentication
failures when 2FA is required on the npm account. actions/setup-node
configures .npmrc to read NODE_AUTH_TOKEN, but the env var must be
explicitly passed to the step.

commit.message:
fix: Book Consultation auto-advances past today when all slots are past

Bug: Arriving at Book Consultation (e.g. from My Pets -> Book Consultation)
showed today's date auto-selected but 'All time slots for this date have
passed' — user had to manually pick tomorrow.

Root cause:
1. autoFindNextDate() used monthly summary status 'available' to pick today,
   but actual time slots for today were all past (evening hours).
2. loadAvailability() fetched slots but never checked 'all past -> advance'.

Fix:
- autoFindNextDate(): Skip today entirely if skipDate=today is passed, or if
  hour >= 23. Accepts optional skipDate param.
- loadAvailability(): After fetching slots, if date is today and all slots
  are past (futureSlots.length === 0 but slots exist), auto-calls
  autoFindNextDate(vetId, today) to skip to the next available date.

Result: Initial load always shows a date with bookable time slots.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove VPC egress, restore project-number API URL

VPC Direct Egress (ALL_TRAFFIC) requires Cloud NAT to reach public IPs.
Without Cloud NAT the subnet has no internet egress path, causing TCP
timeout when the nginx BFF connects to the API's *.run.app endpoint.

Also reverts api_url back to the project-number URL format:
data.google_cloud_run_v2_service.api.uri returns the hash-based v1 URL
(osrs-api-HASH-uc.a.run.app) which resolves to a different IP than the
confirmed-working project-number URL (osrs-api-NUMBER.REGION.run.app).

Security: INGRESS_TRAFFIC_ALL + Cloud Run IAM (OIDC token required from
osrs-ui-sa). IAM auth is enforced at the Cloud Run load balancer before
the container is invoked — unauthenticated external requests get 403
without spinning up the container.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Next: Execute posts and collect user feedback (TASK-119)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Defer eviction commit until after fee-balance validation

Previously, check_and_collect_evictions results were immediately applied
(removing victims from by_hash/seen/account_states) before fee-balance
validation. If the candidate was later rejected for insufficient balance,
the evicted txs were permanently lost from the queue.

Fix: collect evictions speculatively but defer removal until after all
validation passes. Queue-size checks account for pending evictions via
effective_len. Matches stellar-core's tryAdd which only calls
evictTransactions after canAdd succeeds.

Closes #1188

Combines real-time event tracking with advanced predictive analytics. The blade of EL‘druin cannot harm those holding righteous intent.

commit.message:
fix: resolve bugs from comprehensive bug hunting session

- Fix fan chart stacking: use proper band rendering with stackId instead
  of white overlay areas that obscured colored bands
- Fix touch probability off-by-one: check path[0] (starting price) against
  target so below-current targets correctly show ~100% touch probability
- Fix findOptimalTarget/percentiles empty array guard
- Add NaN/Infinity validation to fitGaussianHmm model parameters
- Guard bondCouponEndValue against couponFrequency=0 division by zero
- Guard KantorSidebar spreadPct against division by zero
- Guard VerdictBanner against empty results array
- Debounce sell analysis horizon changes (300ms) to avoid recomputing
  10K Monte Carlo paths on every slider tick
- Fix useRef initial value for React 19 compatibility
- Custom fan chart tooltip showing all percentile values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: comprehensive security system with OTP signup

Backend:
- Add users table with OTP/session token fields
- Add userId column to documents for data isolation
- New auth routes: request-otp, verify-otp, session, logout
- Session token auth (Bearer token from OTP verification)
- Lock API key to fixed admin identity (no impersonation)
- Filter ALL document queries by userId (ownership checks)
- Claude artifacts/edit/prompt endpoints respect ownership
- Legacy docs (userId IS NULL) remain accessible during migration
- CORS locked to explicit origins in production
- MCP auth required in production (no silent skip)
- D1 migration: 0002_security_users.sql

Mobile:
- 2-step OTP login flow (email → 6-digit code → session)
- Session token stored in SecureStore (mobile) / localStorage (web)
- API client uses Bearer token auth (falls back to API key)
- Auto-fill OTP in development mode
- Countdown timer, resend, error states

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add --import-specs and --import-plans CLI flags for folder imports

- Add `--import-specs PATH` to copy all .md files from a directory (or single
  file) into `<workspace>/specs/` and create an initial handoff.md
- Add `--import-plans PATH` to copy all .md files into `<workspace>/plans/`,
  merge-extract tasks into tasks.json, and create handoff.md
- Add `import_folder()` in helpers/import_plan.py with dedicated handoff
  builders for folder-based spec and plan imports
- PromptBuilder already picks up specs/ and plans/ directories automatically
  (from previous change); tasks deduplicated across multiple plan files
- Bump version to 1.0.0rc4
